methodology.summary: End-to-end playbook for 1 1 question bank by tenure that walks an operator from trigger to closed outcome with named artefacts at each step.
Reusable template for 1on1 template managers that codifies the structure, named fields, and decision points so each new instance ships in minutes instead of being re-invented.
Reusable template for 30 day post launch review template that codifies the structure, named fields, and decision points so each new instance ships in minutes instead of being re-invented.
A scoring rubric for acceptance criteria that flags weak ACs before sprint planning, with a fix-pattern table for each weakness type.
Green/yellow/red account health score model for micro-agencies with sub-20 retainer clients, built from observable signals not just NPS.
End-to-end playbook for action item carryover tracker that walks an operator from trigger to closed outcome with named artefacts at each step.
Concrete operating checklist covering agency acquisition prep — the small set of items a practitioner runs every cycle so nothing high-leverage gets skipped.
Reusable template for agency annual plan template that codifies the structure, named fields, and decision points so each new instance ships in minutes instead of being re-invented.
Concrete operating checklist covering agency cash flow friday routine — the small set of items a practitioner runs every cycle so nothing high-leverage gets skipped.
Reusable template for agency kickoff deck template that codifies the structure, named fields, and decision points so each new instance ships in minutes instead of being re-invented.
Concrete operating checklist covering agency pipeline hygiene 15min — the small set of items a practitioner runs every cycle so nothing high-leverage gets skipped.
Micro-agency P&L template with project / retainer / productized revenue lines, contractor costs, and weekly cash-flow + utilization views.
Enterprise-level weekly risk-register template for micro-agencies covering revenue concentration, key-person risk, FX, and contractor classification — refreshable in 30 minutes.
Structured readiness audit for an agency planning a SaaS pivot — IP rights, repeatable assets, codebase ownership, retainer-base stability, founder bandwidth.
Per-task cost modelling, gross margin computation, and pricing-model selection (seat / task / outcome) for LLM-agent products targeting $5-100K MRR.
Methodology for designing autonomous agentic AI systems in products where the core value proposition is autonomous action (auto-triage, auto-scheduling, auto-publishing).
A product methodology for designing and shipping autonomous AI systems — agents that act toward a goal without user-triggering each step.

Two Claude subagents for product scoping work: faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent analyzes competitor features to recommend a minimum feature set for a new product; faion-mlp-agent upgrades an MVP to an MLP (Minimum Lovable Product) through a five-mode sequential pipeline (analyze → find-gaps → propose → update → plan).

Defines, configures, and continuously improves the five Scrum events — Sprint Planning, Daily Standup, Sprint Review, Retrospective, and Backlog Refinement — for a team's specific size, timezone spread, and PM tool.

A decision framework for selecting and tailoring a delivery mode — predictive (waterfall), agile (Scrum/Kanban), or a named hybrid pattern — based on six scored dimensions: requirement clarity, stakeholder engagement, risk tolerance, team agility, contract flexibility, and regulatory burden.

A decision framework for selecting among Predictive (waterfall), Agile (Scrum/Kanban), and Hybrid delivery models based on five project factors: requirements clarity, stakeholder availability, risk tolerance, team experience, and contract type.
End-to-end playbook for ai assisted backlog deduplication that walks an operator from trigger to closed outcome with named artefacts at each step.
Detection protocol for ai assisted velocity anomaly detection — explicit signals, thresholds, and remediation paths so the failure mode is caught before it ships.

Auto-compute PV/EV/AC and SPI/CPI weekly by ingesting Git commit volume, Jira completion, budget burn, and invoice cadence — replacing manual EVM entry with a sensor-driven pipeline that publishes a confidence interval, not a single point estimate.
Evaluation method for ai feature de risking that defines test set, scoring function, pass/fail thresholds, and regression rules so quality is comparable across runs.
Feature-management method for ai feature spec contract — what the brief, gate, and rollout artefacts must contain to move a feature from idea to production safely.
PMBOK 8's first dedicated AI appendix: principles for ethical AI use, AI-assisted decision traceability, and sustainable project delivery.
Framework for applying AI to project risk scoring, schedule variance analysis, resource capacity forecasting, and stakeholder digest generation.
End-to-end playbook for ai leverage estimation model that walks an operator from trigger to closed outcome with named artefacts at each step.
AI integration strategy for products designed around AI from day one.
A product strategy methodology for products where AI is not a feature but the delivery mechanism across all layers (research, design, development, testing, analytics, support).
Runnable recipes that wire Jira → Claude → Slack digest, GitHub PR events → risk register update, and Loom transcripts → retro notes — with code, schemas, and the exact prompt scaffolds the PM ships.
A 2026 survey of AI-augmented PM platforms: Jira+Rovo (enterprise), Monday.
Selection and integration guide for AI-augmented project management tools (Jira+Rovo, ClickUp Brain, Monday.

Ai Risk Aging Agent: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Distressed-project rescue (90-day turnaround)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Ai Sprint Planning Agent: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/AI-assisted sprint planning with capacity reality-check' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Ai Status Digest Pipeline: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Async cross-timezone delivery cadence (P4 outsource)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Anti Theater Retro Guardrails: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'p6-product-dev-team/Bi-weekly retro with mistake-memory feedback' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Architecture Review Cadence Protocol: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Quarterly architecture review cycle' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Async Multi Client Time Boxing: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'p3-technical-freelancer/Freelancer-to-SaaS transition without losing the runway' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Async Standup Methodology: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Async cross-timezone delivery cadence (P4 outsource)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Three-field written-Slack daily standup (Yesterday, Today, Blocker) for 1-3 person cross-timezone teams, with blocker-routing SLA and silence-detection rule.

Audience Okr Template Indie: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'p2-indie-hacker/Build-in-public audience growth: 0 to 5K followers over 6 months' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Microsoft's enterprise project management tool supporting four process templates (Basic, Agile, Scrum, CMMI) with a work-item hierarchy (Epic → Feature → User Story/PBI → Task), configurable Kanban boards, WIP limits, WIQL query language, and full REST API.

Azure DevOps Boards is Microsoft's enterprise project management solution supporting four process templates (Basic, Agile, Scrum, CMMI) with tight integration to Azure Pipelines, Repos, and Test Plans.
Methodology for maintaining a prioritized, healthy backlog that connects work items to product goals.

Maintain a prioritized, healthy list of work connected to product goals using the DEEP principle (Detailed at top, Emergent at bottom, Estimated, Prioritized) and four buckets: Ready, Upcoming, Backlog, Icebox.

Bench Management Tiering: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Build a bench of vetted subcontractors without becoming an agency-of-agencies' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Benefits Realisation Template Outsource: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Project closure with benefits realisation and lessons learned (two weeks)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Identify, quantify, assign ownership for, and track the business value that a project is expected to deliver after go-live.
Benefits realization tracks whether a project's promised business outcomes actually materialize after delivery.

Bid No Bid Scorecard: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Fixed-price bid discovery + estimation (1 week)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Billable vs Internal Allocation Split: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Run a 30-minute resource allocation review' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Billing Rollout Coordination Checklist: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-product-manager/Pricing experiment, hypothesis to result' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Modern AI-era product teams operate as overlapping Venn diagrams rather than relay-race handoffs.
Modern product teams operate as overlapping Venn diagrams rather than relay-race handoffs.

Burndown Diagnosis Cheatsheet: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Update the burndown / burnup and decide one corrective action' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Lightweight per-sprint capacity vs commitment calculator for solo PMs — PTO + ceremonies + interrupt buffer subtracted; outputs a fit ratio used at sprint planning.

Capacity Planning Realistic: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/AI-assisted sprint planning with capacity reality-check' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Capacity vs Ask Balancer: codified delivery-management practice that turns the recurring 'role-product-manager/Quarter planning + OKR cascade' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Route every change request through a formal log → impact analysis → tiered decision → baseline update cycle.
Change control is the formal process that evaluates every proposed change to scope, schedule, cost, or quality against the approved baseline before any work begins.

Change Request Pricing Rubric: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Project from kickoff to client signoff (8–12 weeks)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Change Request Rate Card Template: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/T&M to fixed-price contract conversion (6 weeks)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

ClickUp workspace architecture: 4-level hierarchy (Workspace → Space → Folder → List), ClickApps configuration (Sprints, Time Tracking, Custom Fields, Priorities, Multiple Assignees), custom status workflow per space, views (List, Board, Gantt, Workload, Dashboard), and automation rules for status transitions and template application.
Client Decision Forcing Scripts: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Run an escalation conversation with a stakeholder' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Client Handover Master Checklist: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Handover to client in-house team (3 weeks)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Client Health Scorecard Agency: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Quarterly retainer review per client' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Client Kickoff Script: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Project initiation to first delivery (kickoff to week 4)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Client Maturity Assessment Rubric: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Client onboarding into our delivery cadence (two weeks)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Client Status Email Template Agency: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Weekly client status email batch' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Client Status Report Multistyle: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Async cross-timezone delivery cadence (P4 outsource)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Client Trust Rebuild Comms Templates: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Distressed-project rescue (six-week turnaround)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Client Visibility Vs Velocity Tradeoff: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Async cross-timezone delivery cadence (P4 outsource)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
A structured who-what-when-how matrix (comms/plan.
Plan, execute, and monitor stakeholder communications so that the right people receive the right information at the right time.

Competitive positioning defines how a product is uniquely valuable compared to alternatives — answering "why should customers choose us?" using April Dunford's six-step framework: competitive alternatives → unique attributes → value translation → best-fit customer → market category → positioning statement.
Competitive positioning defines how your product is uniquely valuable compared to alternatives.
Continuous discovery is a weekly cadence of user interviews, feedback synthesis, and opportunity-to-roadmap diffing that keeps product decisions connected to fresh user evidence.
Integrate discovery as a recurring operational cadence rather than a one-time project-start activity.
Teresa Torres' framework: a product trio (PM + Design Lead + Tech Lead) runs weekly customer touchpoints and maps all signals into an Opportunity Solution Tree (OST).
Weekly customer discovery framework where the Product Trio (PM, Design, Tech) maintains an Opportunity Solution Tree.
Contractor Audition Rubric: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Hiring screen / contractor audition' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Contractor Utilization Heatmap: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Friday cash-flow + utilization check' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Structured approach to producing a defensible project cost baseline: bottom-up estimation from a WBS, three-point (PERT) uncertainty modelling, risk-driven contingency reserve, and a separate management reserve.
A bottom-up cost estimation process: decompose the WBS into work packages, apply three-point PERT estimation per package, load labor with a fully-loaded multiplier (1.
Cross Border Tax Cheatsheet: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p3-technical-freelancer/Year-end tax, legal, and cash-flow close' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Canonical handoff protocol (definition-of-ready, artifact list, sign-off mechanism) for BA -> Architect -> Dev -> QA -> DevOps transitions in multi-week product-team flows.

Cross Team Estimation Normalisation: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Multi-team coordination program (continuous, weekly checkpoints)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Cross Team Handoff Tracker: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Run a 30-minute cross-team dependency call' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Cross Team Points Calibration Protocol: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Story-points estimation poker with client team' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Cross Timezone Standup Rotation: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Run a 15-minute daily standup that surfaces blockers, not status' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Six-phase ETL process for migrating a project management tool portfolio between Jira, Linear, Asana, ClickUp, GitHub Projects, Azure DevOps, and others.
Daily Standup Ai Prebrief Template: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p6-product-dev-team/Daily standup with AI pre-brief' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Five-level rubric across PMBoK 7 performance domains so annual delivery-process reviews score on anchored behaviours instead of opinion.
Template for writing a productized-service runbook that lets a senior contractor execute a recurring service end-to-end without founder QA on every step.
Builds, maintains, and queries a cross-task / cross-team dependency graph for multi-team product PMs — "what is at risk if X slips by 1 week" answered deterministically.

Dependency Slack Thresholds delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Run a 30-minute cross-team dependency call' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: When 'slip' becomes 'escalate' is undefined; needed for predictable handoff governan
PR-time design-QA checklist (tokens used, components reused, motion respects prefers-reduced-motion, a11y intact, content rules honored) — gates whether the implementation matches the design.

Distressed Project Diagnostic Script delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Distressed-project rescue (six-week turnaround)' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Rescue work is high-value but corpus has nothing on the diagnostic its
EVM is an objective, formula-driven approach to project performance measurement.

Earned Value Management (EVM) measures project performance by integrating scope, schedule, and cost into three numbers — Planned Value (PV), Earned Value (EV), and Actual Cost (AC) — and derives objective indices (SPI, CPI) and forecasts (EAC, TCPI) that predict final cost and completion date.

Engagement Handover Playbook delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Engagement Handover / Transition Out' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: project-closure exists but is org-internal flavoured (lessons-learned, benefits-realizati
Ladder definitions (L1-L5 with example artifacts), growth-plan template, promo packet template — without it juniors leave.
Scripted openers, de-escalation patterns, written-vs-verbal decision rules, and follow-up commitments for the actual conversation a PM has when a client is unhappy or a vendor missed a date.

Escalation Decision Template delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Run an escalation conversation with a stakeholder' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: stakeholder-engagement-advanced covers theory; missing a 1-pager template th

Estimation Calibration Loop delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'AI-assisted sprint planning with capacity reality-check' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: cost-estimation templates exist; nothing for ongoing estimation calibra
Earned Value variant for fixed-bid outsourcing where revenue is locked but vendor margin tracks against PV/EV/AC at the work-package level — the EVM flavour PMBoK does not document.

Exception Driven Standup Protocol delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Daily standup with AI pre-brief' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Distributed teams waste the most time on status theater. We have ceremony pages but no op
Enterprise experimentation is a disciplined platform — not ad-hoc A/B testing — with defined hypothesis intake, pre-registered metrics, guardrails, SRM checks, and a learning-extraction step.
Run 100+ experiments/year with statistical rigor.
MoSCoW categorizes requirements into Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, and Won't Have buckets for a fixed-timebox release.
Quantitative scoring model that ranks features by `(Reach × Impact × Confidence) / Effort`.
Score each candidate feature as (Reach x Impact x Confidence) / Effort.
Pipeline to turn scattered feedback into product decisions with fixed taxonomy, weighted by segment ARR and retention risk.

Systematic pipeline for collecting feedback from multiple channels, categorizing it against a locked taxonomy, aggregating patterns by topic and segment, linking top clusters to backlog items, and closing the loop with users when their requests ship.

Three-point + Monte Carlo cost recipe tuned for outsource fixed-price bids: every WBS leaf gets O/M/P estimates anchored to senior-dev hourly economics and an AI-agent productivity factor, then 10k-run simulation produces a P85 bid price plus a separate vendor risk reserve.

Fixed Price Vs Tnm Decision Framework delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Fixed-Price vs T&M Estimation with AI Risk Buffer' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Decision rule (when does fixed-price burn margin, when does T&M los

Follow The Sun Handoff Protocol delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Async cross-timezone delivery cadence (P4 outsource)' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Specific to P4 outsource PMs running EU + LATAM or APAC + NA teams. No

Foreign Client Etiquette Playbook delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Foreign-Client Engagement Bootstrap' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Communication patterns differ sharply: US-direct, German-precise, Japanese-indirect,

Foreign Client Kickoff Checklist delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Foreign-client project kickoff (2 weeks)' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Outsource kickoffs across US/EU/UK/AU timezones have a recurring 30+ item checkli
The three PM artefacts a micro-agency founder must keep when there is no PM — and the dozen others to drop without guilt.

Founder Time Audit Tool — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. resource-management methodology covers teams; agencies need a personal-time-allocation audit specifically for the founder bottleneck.

Freelance Capacity Model — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. Solo-operator weekly time-allocation across delivery / pipeline / self / R&D; OKR-setting alone doesn't model the brutal trade-off of 1 person's 40 hours.

Freelance Insurance Buyers Guide — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. E&O / professional liability / cyber insurance is a freelancer blind spot; need a methodology that scopes coverage to the kind of work delivered.

Contractual primitives the P3 freelancer signs every month: MSA + SOW skeleton with deposit, weekly invoicing, IP-transfer-on-payment, warranty clause — and an explicit gap between MSA umbrella terms and per-engagement SOW specifics that keeps both signable in a week.

Freelance Weekly Invoice Cadence — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. 74% don't get paid on time. Need a methodology for weekly invoicing + automated reminders + escalation ladder that doesn't burn the relationship.

Freelancer Client Scorecard — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. portfolio-strategy is product-oriented. Freelancers need a 5-axis client scorecard (rate, payment, scope discipline, growth potential, energy cost) for quarterly rebalance.

Freelancer Handover Bundle Template — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. Concrete checklist + doc template for everything a freelancer must hand over at project end (repo, runbook, creds, future-work, contact-of-record). project-closure is process-level, not artifact-level.
A 30-minute-to-setup personal CRM in Notion / Airtable / plaintext covering leads, active clients, follow-up cadence, and payment reliability for solo freelancers.

Freelancer Proposal Template Fixed Vs Tm — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. Comparison template + decision tree for fixed-scope vs. T&M cap vs. retainer pricing. Bigger PM methodologies cover scope/cost but not the solo's pricing-model choice.

Freelancer Year End Checklist — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. Single-page year-end SOP combining books, taxes, legal, insurance, and planning — none of the existing ops-* methodologies stitch these together for a solo operator.
For teams already on GitHub, GitHub Projects provides native code-task traceability: "Fixes #123" in a PR automatically transitions the linked issue to Done when merged.
GitLab Issue Boards integrate kanban-style project management directly into GitLab's DevOps platform.
GitLab Issue Boards provide kanban-style project management integrated directly with GitLab's DevOps platform.

Handover Pack Template Outsource — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. Project-closure handover templates exist but they are PMI-flavored. Outsource specialist rotation handover is different: usually mid-project, not final close, and aimed at peer or in-house team.
Hybrid delivery is a framework for programs that combine predictive (waterfall) and agile delivery modes when neither alone fits.
A delivery model that combines predictive (waterfall/stage-gate) and agile methods within a single program, assigning each by component risk profile.

Incident Comms Templates Internal External — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. Communications-management is generic. Incidents need ready-to-edit templates for status page, customer email, exec brief, and internal channel; absence costs minutes during outages.
MoR vs direct invoicing, Estonia e-Residency, US LLC for non-residents, VAT MOSS — the pre-first-sale tax/legal checklist with zero faion coverage today.
Structured postmortem template for indie hackers and small-team founders to turn failed launches into public consumption — narrative arc, learning extraction, metrics, opt-in privacy.
Configure and operate Jira projects for Scrum/Kanban/JSM teams: issue type schemes, workflow states/transitions, automation rules, JQL queries, and board configuration.
Jira workflow management covers project setup, issue-type scheme design, workflow state machines, automation rules, and JQL queries for Scrum and Kanban teams.
Alternative agile cadences for continuous-flow teams (Kanban) and multi-team programs (SAFe).
Weekly tech-talk, biweekly demo, monthly architecture review, quarterly skip-level — the rituals that convert tribal knowledge to documented.

Launch Raci Template — testable methodology for delivery, scheduling, RACI, throughput. stakeholder-management covers communication, not the launch-specific RACI with eng / design / marketing / sales / support owners per workstream.

A cross-functional pre-launch readiness review covering 8 gates (security, performance, observability, runbooks, on-call, legal, support enablement, comms tree) that turns implicit launch knowledge into a hard go/no-go decision.
When AI lets anyone clone product features in weeks, the durable competitive advantage is how quickly an org notices changes, updates its beliefs, and ships different answers.
A framework for treating organizational learning velocity as the primary competitive advantage in markets where AI lets competitors clone product experiences in weeks.
Structured knowledge-capture process that runs continuously throughout a project, not only at close-out.
Continuous capture, structured categorisation, and mandatory retrieval of project knowledge so that the same mistakes are not repeated.
Linear is purpose-built for high-velocity software teams.
Linear's opinionated, minimal workflow eliminates configuration overhead — teams are productive in under an hour.
A routing index for all 33 methodologies in pro/product/product-manager/.
A mechanical rubric for cutting any product feature to a day-sized (≤ 8 hours) slice without losing the user value.
Micro-MVPs are extremely small, high-signal experiments designed to validate one key assumption in hours to days rather than weeks.
Micro-MVPs are extremely small, high-signal experiments (hours to days) that validate one specific assumption before committing engineering time.
A selection matrix for choosing one of nine "minimum product" frameworks — MVP, MLP, MMP, MAC, RAT, MDP, MVA, MFP, SLC — before the first spec.
A decision matrix for choosing the right "minimum X product" framework for a given market context.
Minimum Lovable Product planning: a four-layer audit framework (Functional → Reliable → Usable → Delightful) for evolving an MVP that users find "fine" into one they love and recommend.

Plan the upgrade from a validated MVP to a Minimum Lovable Product: a four-layer audit (Functional → Reliable → Usable → Delightful) that identifies the specific gap between "it works" and "I love it".
Monthly Billing Batch Routine: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Monthly invoice + contractor pay batch' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Multi Project Allocation Tracker: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Run a 30-minute resource allocation review' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Define the smallest product version that validates a hypothesis: one core problem, one user, one measurable learning goal, a kill criterion, and Must-Have features capped at 60% of build capacity.

A six-step process for compressing a feature backlog into a minimum viable product: define the core problem, identify the single job the product must do, categorise all features with MoSCoW, validate scope fits the timeline, and define explicit "won't have" and kill-signal criteria.

A working-mode template for digital-nomad solopreneurs that swaps stable-workstation defaults (live deploys, 4-hour focus blocks, real-time pairing) for offline-first, async-only, 2-hour-window work suitable for travel, jet-lag, and 4G hotspots.
Notion's relational databases let teams connect tasks, projects, docs, and meeting notes in one tool.
Notion PM uses interconnected databases (Projects, Tasks, Sprints) with relation and rollup fields to build a customizable agile workspace.
Cascades a single company OKR set into 3-6 squad OKR sets with explicit dependency edges, weekly check-in cadence, and post-quarter retro.
Copy-pasteable OKR cascade template for an 8-10 person multi-role product team — alignment review, contribution mapping, dependency declaration, and quarterly review cadence wired in.
OKRs (Objectives and Key Results) connect inspiring qualitative goals to quantitative progress measures within a fixed period.
OKRs (Objectives and Key Results) combine qualitative inspiring goals (Objectives) with quantitative outcome measures (Key Results).
An outcome-based roadmap replaces feature commitments with measurable metric targets as the unit of planning.

An outcome-based roadmap replaces feature commitments with measurable metric movements — each row states a problem, an outcome ("metric from baseline to target by horizon"), and 2-3 candidate solutions rather than a single committed feature.
Advanced outcome roadmapping closes the gap between high-level business goals and the product experiments that move them.

Decompose a top-line business goal into 2-4 product outcomes, each with a leading indicator measurable within 4 weeks, a confidence level (low/med/high) backed by evidence, and 2-3 candidate experiments.

Outsource Onboarding One Pager Template: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'role-project-manager/Client onboarding into our delivery cadence (two weeks)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Paid Trial Task Library: codified pm practice that turns the recurring 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Hiring screen / contractor audition' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

PMBoK 7 defines eight interconnected performance domains — Stakeholder, Team, Development Approach, Planning, Project Work, Delivery, Measurement, Uncertainty — that together enable successful project delivery.

PMBOK 7 organizes project management into eight concurrent, interconnected performance domains — Stakeholder, Team, Development Approach, Planning, Project Work, Delivery, Measurement, Uncertainty — each focused on outcomes rather than process steps.
Opinionated agenda template for weekly PM ↔ EM, design lead, support lead 1:1s — tuned for product-org reality.
Maps existing project-manager methodology content to the 2026 PMBOK 8 / PMP Examination Content Outline (ECO) domain weights and five exam themes.
Curriculum and content alignment to the 2026 PMP Exam Content Outline (ECO).
The PMP exam form active from July 1, 2026 shifts domain weights significantly: People drops from 42% to 33%, Process from 50% to 41%, and Business Environment rises from 8% to 26% (+18pp).
The PMP exam ECO (Examination Content Outline) changes effective July 1, 2026: People domain drops from 42% to 33%, Process drops from 50% to 41%, and Business Environment rises from 8% to 26%.
PMBoK 8 replaces the rigid five Process Groups with five Focus Areas (Initiating, Planning, Executing, Monitoring and Controlling, Closing) distributed across seven Performance Domains.
PMBOK 8 replaces Process Groups with five method-agnostic Focus Areas — Initiating, Planning, Executing, Monitoring & Controlling, Closing — distributed across 40 non-prescriptive processes.
Structured evaluation framework for selecting or replacing a project management tool.

A structured framework for evaluating and selecting project management tools through a weighted scoring matrix (Core Features 30%, Usability 25%, Integrations 20%, Enterprise 15%, Cost 10%), a two-week proof-of-concept protocol, total cost of ownership (TCO) analysis, and an ADR template for recording the decision.
A structured framework for selecting the right project management tool based on team size, workflow complexity, compliance requirements, and ecosystem fit.

Portfolio EVM Rollup Method — pinned method for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so annual delivery-process maturity review stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

The Three Horizons portfolio model allocates engineering capacity across H1 (Core, 70%), H2 (Adjacent, 20%), and H3 (Transformational, 10%), with ratios adjustable to macro conditions (Growth 60/25/15, Stable 70/20/10, Recession 80/15/5).

Allocates product investment across three horizons: 70% Core (H1, known market, 0-12 months), 20% Adjacent (H2, new segments, 12-24 months), 10% Transformational (H3, breakthrough bets, 24-36 months).

Post Handover Warranty Runbook — pinned runbook for the outsource delivery specialist: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so handover to client in-house team (3 weeks) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Apply machine learning to historical project data to generate early-warning signals for schedule slip, budget overrun, resource contention, and material price risk — turning raw PM telemetry into probability-interval forecasts that change PMO decisions before events occur.

The application of ML-based forecasting to project management: schedule delay early warning, budget overrun detection, resource utilization optimization, and risk pattern mining from issue tracker data.

Six-step framework for engaging external vendors: make-or-buy decision, Statement of Work (SOW) with explicit acceptance criteria, contract type selection (Fixed Price / T&M / Cost Plus), weighted vendor evaluation, contract negotiation, and ongoing vendor management.
Structured vendor engagement: make-or-buy decisions, Statement of Work authoring, contract type selection, vendor evaluation scoring, and ongoing performance monitoring.
Product analytics is measuring and analyzing user behavior to make better product decisions.
Framework for measuring user behavior to drive product decisions.
Product discovery is a time-boxed process (1–4 weeks) for de-risking an idea before committing build resources.

Systematically de-risk ideas before committing engineering capacity by testing four risk types: Value (will customers use it?), Usability (can they figure it out?), Feasibility (can we build it?), and Business Viability (does it work for the business?).
Product explainability is how clearly a product communicates its purpose, value, behavior, and limits to both humans and AI systems.
PM-specific lens: this methodology is a communication discipline, not a marketing artifact.
A product launch is a coordinated introduction of a product to its target market — not just a deployment, but a strategic moment to generate awareness and initial adoption.
A product launch is the coordinated introduction of a product to its target market.

PLG uses the product itself as the primary acquisition, activation, and expansion engine: self-serve onboarding, a clearly defined "aha moment" under 5 minutes, usage-gated upgrade triggers, and PQL scoring that hands off to sales-assist at the right moment.
PLG is a metric-driven loop, not a feature shipping cadence.
A four-stage framework (Introduction → Growth → Maturity → Decline) for diagnosing which lifecycle stage a product is in and applying the matching investment strategy.
Framework for assessing which of four lifecycle stages a product is in (Introduction, Growth, Maturity, Decline) and applying stage-appropriate strategy, metrics, and investments.
A dedicated Product Ops function removes operational drag — process docs, tool plumbing, status updates, metric rollups, stakeholder comms — so PMs spend more time on discovery and strategy.
When a Product Ops function exists, the PM is a consumer and partner — not its operator.

Product Trio Rituals — pinned ritual for the product manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so reset prioritization war with mixed-method scoring stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Program Dependency Aging Chart Recipe — pinned recipe for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so multi-team coordination program (continuous, weekly checkpoints) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Program Weekly Note Template — pinned template for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so multi-team coordination program (continuous, weekly checkpoints) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

A formal 7-step process to end a project: verify deliverable acceptance with signed artefacts, close financial and contract obligations, conduct lessons learned before the team disperses, archive all documentation, produce a final report, hand over to operations, and recognise the team.
Formal termination process for a project: obtain written deliverable acceptance, release resources, close contracts, capture lessons learned, archive documents, and transition to operations.

Project Closure With Lessons Extraction — pinned method for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so distressed-project rescue (90-day turnaround) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.
The PM acts as integrator across all knowledge areas (scope, schedule, cost, quality, risk, resources, communications, procurement) through a single version-controlled integrated plan.
The PM as integrator ensures that decisions in scope, schedule, cost, quality, risk, resources, communications, and procurement are consistent and mutually reinforcing.

Proposal Red Team Checklist — pinned checklist for the outsource delivery specialist: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so fixed-price bid discovery + estimation (1 week) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

A three-process framework (Plan Quality → Manage Quality → Control Quality) applied as code-driven gates: DoD checked per PR by an automated validator, defect metrics aggregated weekly by a collector agent, and severity classified by a triager with human confirmation.

Three-process quality discipline: Plan Quality (define standards and measurable thresholds), Manage Quality / Assurance (ensure processes produce quality), and Control Quality (verify deliverables meet standards via deterministic gates).
A 45-minute scripted quarterly review call for retainer clients that converts utilization data into renewal commitment.

Quit Day Job Trigger Contract — pinned method for the solo SaaS builder: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so $0 → $4k mrr bootstrap journey stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

RACI AI Assisted — pinned method for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so multi-team coordination & dependency-graph reasoning (p6 product) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.
Assign one of four roles — Responsible (does the work), Accountable (single decision owner), Consulted (provides input), Informed (notified after) — to each role for each task or deliverable.
A RACI matrix assigns exactly one of four roles — Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed — to each stakeholder for each task or deliverable.

RAG Policy Thresholds — pinned method for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so write the weekly client status report (p4) / leadership update (p6) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Ramp Task Difficulty Ladder — pinned method for the product-dev team: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so hire + onboard a new product-team dev in 2 weeks stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Quick-reference tables for PMBoK 7 (8 performance domains, 12 principles) and PMBoK 8 (7 domains, 6 principles), plus EVM formulae, estimation accuracy bands, risk response strategies, and RAG status thresholds.

Regulatory Uncertainty Buffer — pinned buffer for the outsource delivery specialist: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so fixed-price vs t&m estimation with ai risk buffer stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.
Structured approach to bundling, scheduling, and communicating product releases across cross-functional teams.
Six-step process for deciding what goes into each release, when it ships, and how it is communicated.

Remote 1 1 Async Fallback — pinned method for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so hold a focused 1:1 with a team member stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.
Effective reporting transforms raw project data into actionable insights.

Automated PM reporting: tool-specific dashboard configuration (Jira, Linear, ClickUp), scheduled report generation for weekly status and sprint reviews, Slack digest delivery, and BI tool integration (PowerBI, Metabase, Grafana) via API ETL.

Identify required skills and quantities per work package, assess real availability (capped at 75-80% effective capacity), map resources to activities, level overloads via delay/split/add/cut options, and monitor utilization weekly against plan.
Plan to 70% utilisation (not 100%), map skills to tasks from a YAML roster in git, level resource load using critical-path analysis, and track allocations weekly against actuals.

Retainer Renewal Decision Rule — pinned decision-rule for the micro-agency founder: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so quarterly retainer review per client stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Retainer Vs Project Rubric — pinned rubric for the micro-agency founder: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so productize one service from custom to fixed stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Retro Action Success Criteria Template — pinned template for the product-dev team: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so bi-weekly retro with mistake-memory feedback stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Retro Facilitation Multistyle — pinned method for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so async cross-timezone delivery cadence (p4 outsource) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Retro Format Rotation Guide — pinned guide for the project manager: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so run sprint review + retrospective back-to-back stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.
Identify threats and opportunities, rate them by probability and impact, assign owners with observable triggers, plan responses, and monitor weekly.
Risk management is a structured process for identifying, scoring, responding to, and monitoring project uncertainty — including both threats (negative risks) and opportunities (positive risks).

A structured log of identified threats and opportunities, each scored by probability x impact (1-25 scale), assigned a response strategy (Avoid/Transfer/Mitigate/Accept for threats; Exploit/Share/Enhance/Accept for opportunities), and owned by a named individual.
A living log of identified threats and opportunities, each with a probability (1–5), impact (1–5), risk score (P×I), chosen response strategy, named owner, trigger condition, and current status.
Pre-agreed policy that defines which risk severity gets escalated to whom, when, and via which channel — closing the gap between "log the risk" and "act on the risk".
A roadmap is a strategic communication tool showing where the product is going and why — not a feature list with dates.
A five-step process for building a product roadmap as a strategic communication tool, not a delivery schedule.
RPO/RTO Negotiation Guide: structures the product-vs-cost conversation between architect and business stakeholders so DR targets are chosen by the buyer, not invented by engineering.
SaaS Stack Audit (Micro-Agency): a 60-minute quarterly review that catches duplicate / unused SaaS tools draining $200-1k/mo from a small services firm.

A deterministic process for building a project schedule from WBS work packages: define activities, sequence with dependency types (FS/FF/SS/SF), estimate durations using three-point PERT, run forward and backward pass to compute Early Start/Early Finish/Late Start/Late Finish per activity, identify the critical path (zero float), and place buffers at merge points and project end using Critical Chain methods rather than per-task padding.
A process for converting WBS work packages into a sequenced, resourced, and buffered schedule with an identified critical path.

AI-assisted classifier that triages every new ticket as legitimate change (→ change-control process) vs scope creep (→ structured pushback) by comparing against the baseline spec, with Jira/Linear webhook integration.
Define, baseline, validate, and control project scope to prevent uncontrolled expansion.
Scope management defines, documents, and controls what a project will and will not deliver.

The five Scrum events — Sprint Planning, Daily Standup, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective, and Backlog Refinement — with time-boxes, facilitation patterns (including async/remote variants), tool configurations, and quality gates.
PMBoK 8 reorganises project management into seven performance domains: Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk.
PMBOK 8 replaces the 10 Knowledge Areas with seven performance domains — Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk — as the primary PM rubric.
Shadow Handover Session Runbook — codifies the offshore→onshore knowledge-transfer pattern (receiver-does, outgoing-watches) so handovers stop relying on tribal memory.
Personal-runway math: monthly burn vs MRR ramp, when to leave a job, geo-arbitrage budget — life/business hybrid doc indie hackers need.
PMBoK 8 consolidates the PMBoK 7 twelve principles into six: Adopt Holistic View, Focus on Value, Embed Quality, Lead Accountably, Integrate Sustainability, Build Empowered Teams.

PMBOK 7 defines six guiding principles for every project management decision: Adopt Holistic View, Focus on Value, Embed Quality, Lead Accountably, Integrate Sustainability, and Build Empowered Teams.
Small Team Comms Rhythm — a 2-to-3-person comms cadence (founder + 1-2 ICs, no scrum master) sized for micro-agencies and indie teams where formal PM artefacts are overkill.
Quantitative burnout-early-warning signals (sleep, weekend creep, MRR-to-effort drift) for solo founders — fires before the breakdown, not after.
One-page change-order template a freelancer emails when the client says "just one more thing" — signed within an hour, billable from that hour, no MSA renegotiation.
Opinionated mode-batching protocol for the solo founder day — separates building from inbox/support/ops to cut context-switch cost from hours to minutes.
Boilerplate contract clauses — late-fee schedule, work-pause right, re-engagement terms — that a freelancer can drop into a SOW or master agreement.

Hour-by-hour operational runbook for a solo founder's launch day — Stripe live, monitoring armed, mailer warm, fallback content queued, support inbox, social burst — so the one operator does not improvise on the most expensive day of the quarter.
One-sheet MRR / churn / LTV dashboard spec calibrated for a one-operator SaaS — five canonical metrics, exact Stripe-API formulas, no finance team required.
Opinionated pivot taxonomy (segment / feature / business-model / tech / channel) with triggers, sunk-cost guardrails, and a hard rule for distinguishing a pivot from quitting.

Solo-specific effective-hourly calculator that loads target income with taxes, downtime, marketing time, benefits, and tooling cost to produce a defensible rate floor below which engagements are mathematically unprofitable.
Habit-plus-tooling discipline so the solo's billable hours are captured at the moment of work and Friday reconciliation collapses from 2h to 15min.
Lightweight utilization-and-pipeline dashboard (spreadsheet or Notion) for a freelance practice — drives rate decisions and prevents feast-or-famine.
Sunday-review + Monday-prime weekly cadence template tailored for a one-operator product — fixed artifacts, memory hooks, time-boxed.
A product specification (PRD) defines WHAT to build and WHY, leaving HOW to design and engineering.

Sprint demo script for the senior-dev-runs-the-demo-for-exec-client variant — explicit per-AC accept/reject prompt, owner-named scope-cut decisions, and a decision log entry the engagement manager signs.
Compact three-part sprint-goal formula (outcome + boundary + measure) that produces a single sentence the team can recite, used to filter scope additions and to grade the sprint at review.

A fill-in-the-blank template that compresses a sprint into one outcome sentence ("In sprint NN, we will <verb> <user/system> so that <measurable benefit>, focusing on <surface>, deferring <out-of-scope>"), recitable by every team member at standup.

Explicit disagree-and-commit protocol for product roadmap decisions — named-dissent capture, escalation thresholds, and a time-boxed revisit trigger that prevents stakeholder drift from quietly unwinding the roadmap mid-quarter.

A structured process to identify all parties affected by or affecting a project, map each on the Power/Interest grid, assign an engagement strategy per quadrant, and execute a communication cadence — stored as version-controlled YAML register so changes produce a diff history and evidence-backed attitudes.
Systematic identification, classification, and engagement of everyone who affects or is affected by the project.
A structured process for moving stakeholders from their current engagement level (Unaware, Resistant, Neutral, Supportive, Leading) to a desired level through targeted, evidence-based activities.
Stakeholder lists without engagement strategies produce transactional relationships where PMs reach out only when they need something.

Stakeholder management identifies everyone affected by a product (end users, decision makers, influencers, builders, support, external), maps them on a Power/Interest grid with an explicit attitude column (Supporter/Neutral/Resistor), assigns a named owner and engagement cadence per stakeholder, and codifies approval gates so agents can block SDD transitions until named approvers sign off.
Stakeholder management is identifying everyone affected by your product, mapping them by power and interest, and running a systematic engagement cadence.

Identify every person or group who affects or is affected by the project, analyze their influence (power) and interest, classify into Power-Interest quadrants, and define an engagement strategy per quadrant.
Hidden stakeholders block projects at the worst possible moments.

Methodology for an ongoing per-stakeholder sentiment trend (supportive / cautious / hostile) built from emails, chat threads, and meeting transcripts — gives the PM early warning weeks before a sponsor goes red.
Audience-segmented status report templates — CEO, PMO, technical sponsor, internal leadership — that replace the one-size-fits-all weekly status email with reports each audience will actually read.
End-to-end Steering Committee (Steerco) playbook — pre-meeting prep, in-meeting facilitation, post-meeting follow-up — calibrated for outsource-PM monthly and product-PM quarterly cadences.
Team Charter Working Agreement: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p6-product-dev-team/Adopt faion org-wide and override with company patterns'.

A structured approach to building high-performing teams using Tuckman's five-stage model (Forming, Storming, Norming, Performing, Adjourning), a team charter with working agreements, a skills matrix for gap planning, and retrospective cycles for continuous improvement.

A framework for building high-performing project teams using Tuckman's stages (Forming → Storming → Norming → Performing → Adjourning) as the diagnostic backbone, paired with three artifact types: team charter, skills matrix, and retrospective template.
A 4-question anonymous pulse survey (eNPS + workload + autonomy + clarity) run mid-sprint to catch morale slides before they turn into attrition or distressed-project signals.

A six-step framework for making technical debt visible, quantified, and systematically paid down — covering debt registration, impact scoring (interest × contagion / effort), capacity allocation, and prevention policies.
Systematic process for making technical debt visible, quantifying its interest cost, prioritizing paydown against the roadmap, and allocating 15-20% of sprint capacity continuously.
Timezone Overlap Comms Charter: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p4-outsource-specialist/Foreign-Client Engagement Bootstrap'.
Timezone Overlap Handoff Protocol: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p4-outsource-specialist/Cross-timezone async daily standup'.
Tiny Bets Quarterly Cadence: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p2-indie-hacker/Multi-product portfolio rotation: ship N small bets per year'.
Tm To Fp Conversion Playbook: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p4-outsource-specialist/T&M to fixed-price contract conversion (6 weeks)'.
Tool Consolidation Decision Rule: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p5-micro-agency-founder/Vendor / tool consolidation review'.
A structured approach to migrating project management data between tools, covering pre-migration audit, field mapping, ETL execution, and post-cutover validation.

A phase-by-phase execution playbook for migrating project data between PM tools at org scale: six phases (Planning → Preparation → Pilot → Full Migration → Cutover → Stabilization) with an ETL engine, cutover checklist, rollback strategy, and change-management comms plan.

A visual, card-based kanban workflow on Trello: board structure with WIP-limited lists, card anatomy with labels and checklists, Butler automation for rule-based transitions, custom fields for story points and sprint tracking, and REST API integration for agent-driven card management.
Trello is a card-based kanban tool where work items move left-to-right through 5–7 named lists.

User story mapping arranges stories into a two-dimensional model: user activities as the horizontal backbone (left-to-right = user journey) and tasks stacked vertically by priority within each activity.
Arrange user stories into a two-dimensional map: backbone activities run left-to-right (user journey), tasks are stacked vertically under each activity by priority.

Map the end-to-end flow from customer request to customer value, measure it with Flow Metrics (Lead Time, Cycle Time, Throughput, WIP, %C/A) and DORA (Deployment Frequency, Change Lead Time, Change Failure Rate, MTTR), identify the constraint per Theory of Constraints, and run targeted experiments to elevate that constraint.
Value Stream Management (VSM) maps the end-to-end path from customer request to delivered value and measures flow using Lead Time, Cycle Time, Process Time, %Complete/Accurate, and Throughput.
Scoring matrix + trial protocol + rollback gate for in-house team buying $50-500/mo SaaS (Datadog vs Grafana, Linear vs Jira, Vercel vs Netlify).
Vendor Management Pm: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-project-manager/Multi-team coordination & dependency-graph reasoning (P6 product)'.

A 6-pattern checklist that detects silent margin-eroding behaviours (silent scope creep, free analysis, missing change requests, AI rework loops, gold-plating, sympathy discounting) before they consume 10-20% of fixed-price project margin.
Vendor Risk Assessment Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p6-product-dev-team/SOC2 / GDPR audit prep (annual)'.
Bridges Mom-Test verbatim customer quotes to prioritised backlog cards via a 4-step transform (extract → cluster → frame → prioritise) that keeps the customer's words attached to every story.
Warranty Period Sop: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p3-technical-freelancer/Project kickoff to handover (typical 6-12 week engagement)'.
Hierarchical decomposition of project scope into deliverable-oriented work packages using the 100% rule.
Without a WBS, estimates are guesses and dependencies are invisible until they block progress.
Weekly Status Report Outsource Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p4-outsource-specialist/Weekly client status report'.
A deliverable-oriented hierarchical decomposition of project scope into work packages — noun-based nodes (outputs), not verbs (activities).
A hierarchical decomposition of total project scope into deliverable-oriented (noun-led) work packages.
Two core PM pipelines: Project Bootstrap (idea → constitution → TASK_000) and MLP Planning (MVP → Most Lovable Product).
